Saturday, April 11, 2026 Daily Digest

Canva Acquires Its Way to Full Marketing Stack, Accenture Bets on Replit, Enterprise AI Scales Beyond Pilots

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

Canva Acquires Simtheory + Ortto to Build End-to-End Marketing Platform

Canva is no longer just a design tool. With the acquisitions of Simtheory (AI agent collaboration) and Ortto (customer data + marketing automation), Canva is making a major play into campaign execution. The vision: create, deploy, and optimize campaigns without leaving the platform. This is a direct shot at Adobe's marketing cloud — with Canva's signature simplicity.

Read on DailyOilFutures →
2

Accenture Makes Strategic Investment in Replit for Enterprise AI Dev

Accenture is betting on Replit's AI-assisted development platform to accelerate software creation for enterprise clients. The partnership signals that AI coding tools are moving from developer toys to core enterprise infrastructure. Accenture will adopt Replit internally while helping clients integrate AI into their dev workflows.

Read on StartupNews →
3

Enterprise AI Matures: Companies Now Averaging 28 Autonomous Systems

The pilot phase is officially over. IT leaders now report an average of 28 autonomous or semi-autonomous AI systems in operation — and plan to scale to 40 within the year. The primary driver has shifted from efficiency to speed-to-market. Most barriers are organizational: fragmented data, siloed teams, and unclear ROI frameworks. Only 16% have redesigned jobs around AI.

Read on AI Daily →
4

"The AI Hangover Is Over" — Wrapper Startups Are Dead

A blunt reality check: the thousands of "ChatGPT wrapper" startups that cropped up in 2023-24 have mostly evaporated. Smart enterprises learned the lesson — dependency on generic APIs is a liability. The pivot now is toward vertical integration: fine-tuned models, proprietary data lakes, and strict guardrails. Hard work, but the only defensible moat.

Read on Techaeris →
5

2026: The Year of Agentic Marketing Automation

Forrester's prediction is playing out: 60% of marketing automation platforms now include native generative AI. The bigger shift: agentic AI systems that don't just execute workflows but actively plan, test, and adapt campaigns with minimal human instruction. We're moving from "AI-assisted" to "AI-autonomous" — and the ROI math is finally becoming defensible to CFOs.

Read on ArticlEdge →

💡 My Take

Watch Canva. They're doing to marketing what they did to design — making enterprise-grade capabilities accessible without the enterprise-grade complexity (or price tag). If they execute on the Simtheory + Ortto vision, Adobe and Salesforce should be nervous. The "create → execute → optimize" loop in one platform is exactly what time-starved marketing teams want.
